stories.title: Learning to Ride a Bike
stories.story:
When Hana turned eight, her parents gave her a shiny blue bicycle for her birthday. She was thrilled, until she actually tried to ride it. The moment she pushed off, the bike wobbled, and she fell right onto the grass.

"This is impossible," Hana said, rubbing her knee. Her older brother, Youssef, sat down beside her. "It took me weeks to learn," he admitted. "Nobody rides perfectly on the first try."

Every weekend after that, Youssef held the back of Hana's bike as she pedaled down their street. She fell more times than she could count, scraping her elbows and knees. Some days, she wanted to give up completely and leave the bicycle in the garage forever.

But Youssef never let her quit for the day feeling defeated. "One more try," he always said. Then, one Saturday morning, something clicked. Hana pedaled forward, and Youssef let go without her noticing. She rode all the way to the end of the street by herself, wind in her hair, laughing with pure joy. When she finally looked back and realized she had done it alone, she felt like she could do anything.

stories.title: The New Student
stories.story:
A new boy joined Mariam's class in the middle of the school year. His name was Tarek, and he sat alone at lunch every single day, staring quietly at his food. Nobody talked to him, and he didn't talk to anyone either.

Mariam noticed this and felt a small tug of sadness for him. She remembered how nervous she had felt on her very first day of school, years ago. One lunchtime, she decided to walk over and sit across from him.

"Hi, I'm Mariam," she said simply. Tarek looked up, surprised that someone had actually come to sit with him. "I'm Tarek," he replied quietly. "I just moved here from another city. I don't know anyone."

Mariam smiled and started asking him questions about his old school, his hobbies, and his favorite games. Slowly, Tarek relaxed and even laughed at one of her jokes. By the end of lunch, they had discovered they both loved the same cartoon show.

From that day on, Tarek always sat with Mariam and her friends. What started as one small act of kindness turned into a real, lasting friendship — one that made a big, unfamiliar school feel a little more like home for both of them.
